script.topic: The information vacuum every D2C founder is operating in right now
script.hook: Your competitor's best-performing Meta ad is live right now. You just can't see it. Or can you?
script.body:
You're tweaking your headline at 11pm.
Testing a new creative.
Adjusting your budget again.

Meanwhile your top competitor ran 6 ad variations last week, found their winner on day 3, and scaled it hard.

You have no idea that happened.

That's not a skills problem.
That's not a budget problem.
That's an information problem.

Most D2C founders are optimizing in complete isolation — running tests, burning spend, iterating on instinct — while the answers they're looking for are already sitting inside their competitors' active ad accounts.

There's a tool Meta built that shows you every ad any brand is running right now.

It's called the Meta Ad Library.
It's public.
It's free.
And almost nobody in your space is actually using it strategically.

Not because they don't know it exists.
Because pulling meaningful intelligence from it — angles, hooks, offer structures, creative patterns, what's scaling vs. what's being killed — takes hours of manual work most founders don't have.

So they skip it.
And they keep optimizing blind.

We built a tool that does that work for you.

Fill out a Google Form with your competitor's brand name.
Our system scrapes their Meta Ad Library data, runs it through AI analysis, and delivers a 17-section intelligence report to your inbox.

In under 10 minutes.

Not a summary. Not a screenshot. A structured breakdown:
— Active ad hooks and angles
— Offer and CTA patterns
— Creative format distribution
— What they're testing vs. what they're scaling
— Gaps in their strategy you can move into

Before you run your next test, it might be worth knowing what tests your competitor already ran.

Question for the founders in the comments:

How many of you have actually pulled your top competitor's active ads this week?
script.cta: Comment 'INTEL' and I'll show you what your competitor's ad strategy looks like right now.
script.caption:
Your competitor's best-performing Meta ad is live right now. You just can't see it. Or can you?

Most D2C founders are optimizing in complete isolation — tweaking copy, testing creatives, adjusting budgets — while their competitors have already solved those exact problems and are scaling the winners.

The Meta Ad Library is public. Free. Updated in real time. And almost no one is using it the way they should.

We built a tool that scrapes it for you and delivers a 17-section AI-powered intelligence report on your top competitor's entire Meta ad strategy — in under 10 minutes.

Hooks. Angles. Offer structure. Creative patterns. What they're testing. What they're scaling. Where their gaps are.

Before you run your next test, know what tests your competitor already ran.

Comment 'INTEL' and I'll show you what your competitor's ad strategy looks like right now.

script.topic: Contrarian take — why testing your own ads without competitor data is a trap
script.hook: We tested ads and they didn't work — said every founder who never looked at what their competitors were running.
script.body:
You blamed the creative.
Then the copy.
Then the targeting.
Then the algorithm.
You increased the budget to 'give it more data.'

Nothing converted.

So you launched another round of tests.

This is the trap.

Here's what actually happened:

While you were A/B testing headline variations, your top competitor was on week 14 of running the same UGC testimonial video.

Same creative. 14 weeks straight.

Do you know what it means when a brand runs the same ad for 14 weeks on Meta?

It converts. Consistently. At a margin that makes it worth every dollar of continued spend.

That's not luck. That's signal.

A real scenario:

A D2C skincare brand tests 6 creatives over 3 weeks. None of them hit. CPAs are brutal. The founder pulls the plug, blames iOS, and starts over.

Meanwhile, their closest competitor — same price point, same target demo — has been running a single 47-second founder story video since January.

The founder story angle. Uncontested in that niche. Right there in the Meta Ad Library. Publicly visible. Free to observe.

The data was available the entire time.

This is the gap most founders are operating in:

They treat ad testing like it exists in a vacuum.
Launch. Measure. Kill. Repeat.

But testing without competitive context isn't optimization.
It's guessing with a budget.

The brands winning right now on Meta aren't just better at creative.
They know what's already working in their category.
They're not starting from zero.

The Meta Ad Library is public.
Every active ad.
Every competitor.
Every angle, offer, and hook they're currently scaling.

The information asymmetry isn't because the data doesn't exist.
It's because most founders aren't reading it — and definitely aren't reading it systematically.

That's the first fix.

Before you launch your next round of tests:
Know what the winners in your category are running right now.

Stop testing in the dark.

script.topic: Build in public — why I built this and what problem it actually solves
script.hook: I built a pipeline that scrapes the Meta Ad Library, feeds it to Claude, and delivers a 17-section competitor intelligence PDF in under 10 minutes. Here's why I built it.
script.body:
VARIANT A — PAIN-FIRST [SELECTED WINNER]

---

I built a pipeline that scrapes the Meta Ad Library, feeds it to Claude, and delivers a 17-section competitor intelligence PDF in under 10 minutes.

Here's why I built it.

---

I kept watching the same thing happen.

A D2C founder spends ₹80K testing creatives.
Nothing lands.
They kill the campaign.
Try a new angle.
Spend again.

Meanwhile — their top competitor has been running the same ad for 63 days straight.

Same hook. Same offer. Same visual structure.
63 days.

That is not a coincidence. That is a winning ad.
And it has been publicly visible in the Meta Ad Library the entire time.

Unread.

---

Here's what most founders don't know:

If a brand is spending money to keep an ad live for 30, 60, 90 days — it is converting.
Meta's own infrastructure is telling you what's working.
You just don't have a system to read it.

The intelligence was never hidden.
The process to extract it was just slow, manual, and inconsistent enough that nobody actually did it.

---

So I built the process.

You fill a Google Form with your brand name and niche.
A scraper pulls your top competitors' active Meta ads.
Claude analyzes the output across 17 dimensions — hooks, offer structure, creative angles, ad longevity, emotional triggers, format patterns, and more.
A structured PDF lands in your inbox.

Under 10 minutes. No agency. No waiting weeks for a competitive audit that's already outdated by the time it arrives.

---

This is not a finished product.

I'm going to say that clearly: this is in beta.

I've run it internally. I know what it can do. But I want to pressure-test it against real brands, real niches, real competitors — before I scale it.

So here's what I'm doing:

I'm giving it away free to 5 D2C founders.

All I ask in return is honest feedback.
What was useful. What was missing. What you'd actually pay for.

---

If you're running Meta ads right now and making creative decisions without knowing what your competitors are actively spending on —

This post is the DM you were supposed to send.

---

👇 DM me your brand name and niche.
I'll run the report and send it to you free.
Five spots. That's it.

script.production_notes:
VARIANT REFERENCE LOG — for internal review:

VARIANT A (WINNER — Pain-First): Hook lands on the exact failure moment — founder bleeding ad spend while competitor's winning creative sits publicly visible and ignored. Origin story builds credibility. Beta framing increases trust, not doubt. Scarcity of 5 spots drives DM urgency. Predicted highest DM inquiry rate.

VARIANT B (Result-First — Not Selected): Would open with 'In under 10 minutes, you'll know every hook, angle, and offer your top 3 competitors are running on Meta right now.' Then explain the pipeline and the beta offer. Strong on LinkedIn for awareness, but on a build-in-public post the narrative journey outperforms the result-first lead — founders want to understand why you built it before they trust the outcome claim. Lower predicted save rate than Variant A.

VARIANT C (Pattern Interrupt — Not Selected): Would open with 'Your competitors are running the same ad they launched 60 days ago. That ad is still live. That means it's working. You just haven't looked.' Strong scroll-stop on LinkedIn, but slightly more appropriate for a standalone awareness post, not a build-in-public narrative. The curiosity gap closes too fast without enough founder empathy to drive DM action on this specific post type.

FORMATTING NOTES: Use line breaks aggressively. LinkedIn's algorithm rewards dwell time — white space forces slower reading. The horizontal rule (---) acts as a visual pause and section separator, mimicking newsletter structure which performs well with the agency/founder audience on LinkedIn. Do not compress paragraphs. Each beat needs its own breathing room.

NO human face or voice required. Pure text post. Zero production overhead.

script.topic: Pattern interrupt — what 90 days of running the same ad actually means
script.hook: If your competitor has been running the same Meta ad for 90 days, congratulations. They just handed you their most profitable creative for free.
script.body:
Here's a rule that every media buyer knows but most brand owners don't:

No one runs a losing ad for 90 days.

If a brand is keeping the same creative live for months, it's because that creative is converting. Profitably. Consistently.

That's not an assumption. That's how paid advertising works.

Ad spend is real money. Nobody burns it on something that isn't working.

So when you see a competitor running the same hook, the same offer, the same visual for 90+ days — you're not looking at stubbornness.

You're looking at their most profitable asset.

And here's the part that should stop you mid-scroll:

The Meta Ad Library is public.

Every active ad. Every inactive ad. Launch dates. Run duration. The exact creative.

All of it. Free. Right now.

Your competitor's best-performing ad is sitting there, visible to anyone who knows where to look.

Almost no D2C founder is checking this systematically.

Not because it's hard.

Because nobody built a workflow around it.

Until now.

We built OffGrid Creatives AI to do exactly this.

Fill out a Google Form with your competitor's brand name.

Our system scrapes the Meta Ad Library, runs the data through AI, and delivers a 17-section Ad Intelligence Report directly to your inbox.

In under 10 minutes.

You get: which ads are running, how long they've been live, what angles they're testing, what offers they're pushing, and where the gaps are that you can exploit.

This is the competitive layer that most founders are completely skipping.

We're in beta. A small number of brands are getting early access this week.

Want to see what your competitor's 90-day ads look like?

DM me your niche and I'll pull it for you — free, this week only.

posting_rules: Every LinkedIn post must be written in first-person founder voice — no third-person brand references, no corporate language.
Every Instagram carousel must be designed to be saved — each slide should deliver standalone value readable as a screenshot.
No post should mention the product without first naming the problem it solves — lead with the gap, close with the product.
Hook must appear as the first line of every post with no preamble — no 'Hey everyone', no 'I wanted to share'.
Instagram Stories must be posted 5 days per week — they are the primary DM funnel entry point and must always include a response mechanic (poll, question box, swipe up, DM prompt).
Every CTA must be singular — one action per post, never two or three competing calls to action.
DM keyword triggers (INTEL, REPORT, SIGNALS, EDGE, BETA) must be consistent across platforms and tracked manually in a response log.
LinkedIn posts should be between 150 and 400 words — long enough to deliver value, short enough to read in one scroll.
Instagram captions should be between 3 and 6 lines — hook in line 1, value in lines 2-3, CTA in final line.
Reels must open on the hook within the first 2 seconds — no logo reveals, no music intros, no slow pans.
Never post a product-first piece of content — every post must earn the right to mention OffGrid by delivering value first.
Build-in-public posts must include specific details — numbers, timelines, decisions made, things that didn't work. Vague transparency is not transparency.

If a beta report has not been delivered yet when a proof-seeds post is scheduled, reframe the post around process transparency or a finding from the Meta Ad Library itself — never fabricate testimonials or reactions.
Cross-post strategically — LinkedIn text posts can be adapted for Instagram captions but must be reformatted for platform norms. Do not copy-paste verbatim across platforms.
what_not_to_post: Generic ad tips that any marketing account could post — 'use strong CTAs', 'test your creatives', 'know your audience'.
Motivational quotes or founder mindset content unrelated to the product's core gap.
Polls asking 'which do you prefer, A or B?' without connecting the answer back to competitive intelligence or the product.
Content that references AI without being specific — never say 'we use AI' without saying which AI, what it does, and what the output looks like.
Oversaturated stat-backed content with no original insight — '82% of marketers say...' type posts that appear across thousands of generic accounts.
Fake urgency or manufactured scarcity — every beta limit, deadline, or spot count must be real.
Content that positions OffGrid as a tool rather than a practice — the product enables a competitive intelligence practice, it is not just another SaaS tool.
Posts that lead with the price before establishing value — INR 999 is only mentioned after the value case has been made.
Agency-tone content — no 'we help brands scale' language, no stock imagery of charts and dashboards, no before/after graph graphics without real data.
Content that apologizes for being in beta — beta is a feature, not a flaw. Frame it as early access and proof-in-progress, never as unfinished or incomplete.

strategy.strategic_angle: GAP ANGLE — You are the only voice telling D2C founders to stop building ad strategy blind and start reverse-engineering competitor intelligence from the Meta Ad Library. No one in the scraped feed is saying this. You own this narrative from day one.

phase_1.name: PROOF FACTORY
phase_1.goal: Generate 3-5 completed beta reports, collect 3+ written testimonials or video reactions, build initial audience trust on Instagram and LinkedIn with founder-POV content that names the competitor-intelligence gap
phase_1.content_focus: Founder-POV narrative posts naming the structural gap (founders are building ad strategy in an information vacuum), behind-the-scenes build-in-public showing the pipeline working in real time, and free/discounted beta report drops to 5 hand-picked D2C founders in exchange for honest public feedback
key_actions: Identify 20 D2C founders and agency owners on LinkedIn and Instagram who have posted about Meta ads frustration — DM them a free beta report in exchange for a public testimonial or 30-second video reaction

Publish 3 LinkedIn posts using the exact hooks from trend data: 'Your competitor's best-performing Meta ad is live right now. You just can't see it.' and 'We tested ads and they didn't work — said every founder who never looked at what their competitors were running.'
Create one Instagram carousel: 'What the Meta Ad Library tells you that your agency won't' — educational, positions OffGrid as the authority naming the gap
Record a 60-second Loom or Reel walking through a real (anonymized) beta report — show the 17 sections, the speed of delivery, the depth of analysis — post natively on both platforms

Post a build-in-public thread on LinkedIn: 'I built a pipeline that scrapes the Meta Ad Library, feeds it to Claude, and delivers a 17-section competitor intelligence PDF in under 10 minutes. Here's what happened when I gave it to 5 D2C founders for free.'
Collect every piece of feedback — screenshots of DMs, testimonial quotes, reaction videos — and store in a proof bank for Phase 2
Join 3-5 relevant WhatsApp/Telegram communities for D2C founders and agency owners — contribute value before ever mentioning the product, then soft-drop the beta offer by week 3
phase_1.success_gate: Minimum 3 completed beta reports delivered AND minimum 2 public testimonials (screenshot, quote, or video) collected. If gate is not met by Day 30, extend Phase 1 by 10 days before moving to Phase 2. Do not scale content volume without proof assets.

phase_2.name: CONTENT WEAPONIZATION
phase_2.goal: Convert proof into high-frequency content, reach 3-5 paying beta clients at INR 999, grow audience to 500+ engaged followers across platforms, establish OffGrid as the singular voice for competitor ad intelligence
phase_2.content_focus: Before/After comparison posts (blind strategy vs intelligence-led strategy), testimonial-embedded carousels, stat-backed posts using real data from delivered reports (anonymized), Q1/Q2 performance narrative posts, and direct CTA content driving to Google Form
key_actions: Turn every testimonial into a native content piece — screenshot post with founder caption on Instagram, narrative post on LinkedIn with the story of what the report revealed for that founder

Publish the Before/After carousel format: 'Founder X was spending 40k/month on Meta ads blind. Here's what their competitor intelligence report revealed in 10 minutes.' — use real anonymized data from beta reports

Launch a 'Competitor Ad Teardown' content series on Instagram — pick one well-known D2C brand per week, show 3 insights from their Meta Ad Library presence, end with CTA: 'Want this for YOUR competitors? Link in bio.'
Create and pin a LinkedIn article or long post: 'Why 90% of D2C founders are building ad strategy in an information vacuum — and the 10-minute fix' — this becomes the evergreen trust anchor
Activate the INR 999 beta pricing publicly — frame it as limited to first 10 clients, create genuine scarcity since it is genuinely scarce
Run a '48-hour beta drop' twice during this phase — announce on Stories and LinkedIn that 3 spots are open at beta price, close the form after 48 hours or 3 fills, whichever comes first
Engage daily in comments on posts by D2C founders, agency owners, and e-commerce communities — always add value, never spam, but have a profile that clearly communicates what OffGrid does
phase_2.success_gate: Minimum 3 paying beta clients at INR 999 AND minimum 300 combined followers across Instagram and LinkedIn AND at least 2 content pieces that crossed 50+ organic engagements. If gate is not met by Day 60, audit content resonance, re-survey beta clients on messaging, and adjust hooks before Phase 3.

phase_3.name: CONVERSION ENGINE
phase_3.goal: Close remaining 5-7 paying clients to hit 10 total, begin price ladder testing toward INR 3500, build retargeting-ready content library for future paid amplification, validate repeatable conversion path
phase_3.content_focus: Social proof stacking (multiple testimonials in single posts), ROI-framed content ('This founder found 3 competitor angles they'd never tested — cost: INR 999, time: 10 minutes'), urgency-driven beta closing content, and early authority content positioning OffGrid for post-beta pricing

weekly_output.micro_budget_boosted_posts: 1-2 per week if any budget unlocked from beta revenue (INR 500-1000 per boost)

key_actions: Stack all testimonials into a single 'Wall of Proof' carousel — post on both platforms, pin to profile

Publish a '10 things we learned analyzing competitor ads for X founders' post using aggregated anonymized insights from all delivered reports — positions OffGrid as having proprietary data no one else has
Announce beta closing: 'We're closing INR 999 pricing after 10 clients. 4 spots left.' — update the count publicly as clients come in
Run a LinkedIn poll or Instagram Story poll: 'Do you know what your top 3 competitors are running on Meta right now? Yes / No / Wait you can check that?' — use results as content and lead-gen
DM every person who engaged with content in Phase 2 but did not convert — personal message from founder, not a template, referencing their specific business
If beta revenue allows (INR 3000-5000 total), boost the single highest-performing Instagram post or Reel from Phase 2 with a micro-budget targeting D2C founder interests in India
Begin testing INR 3500 pricing with 1-2 clients who come in after the beta closes or who have higher willingness to pay — do not announce publicly yet, test via DM conversations
Document the full 90-day journey in a long-form LinkedIn post or Twitter thread — this becomes the launch content for the post-beta phase and attracts the next wave of clients
phase_3.success_gate: 10 total paying clients across all phases AND at least 1 client acquired at INR 3500 or willingness-to-pay signal at that tier AND a documented conversion path (content type → DM or form → payment → delivery → testimonial) that can be repeated in Month 4+.

platform_priority: LinkedIn — primary for Phase 1 because founder-to-founder trust content performs better here, D2C founders and agency owners are active, and long-form narrative builds credibility faster than Instagram in zero-proof stage

Instagram — primary for Phase 2 and co-primary for Phase 3 because visual proof formats (carousels, before/after, Reels) scale reach faster once proof assets exist, and the target hashtag ecosystem (#metacads, #d2cmarketing, #facebookads) has high posting volume but near-zero quality competition
WhatsApp/Telegram D2C communities — secondary channel throughout, used for relationship-building and soft beta drops, not primary content distribution
content_pillars: THE GAP — Content that names the problem no one else is naming: D2C founders are spending money on Meta ads without ever looking at what competitors are running. This is the structural content gap identified in trend data. Every piece in this pillar educates the audience that competitor ad intelligence exists and they're ignoring it.

THE PROOF — Content built from real beta report deliveries: testimonials, anonymized data insights, before/after narratives, founder reaction screenshots. This pillar directly attacks the trust bottleneck.

THE TEARDOWN — Educational content where OffGrid publicly analyzes a well-known brand's Meta Ad Library presence, reveals 2-3 strategic insights, and ends with a CTA. This demonstrates product value before purchase and exploits the 'What the Meta Ad Library tells you that your agency won't' format from trend data.

THE BUILD — Behind-the-scenes founder-POV content showing the pipeline, the AI analysis process, the speed of delivery. This pillar uses the 'I noticed / I checked / here's what I found' narrative arc identified in trending formats.

THE CLOSE — Direct conversion content with clear CTA, scarcity framing (limited beta spots), and price anchoring (INR 999 vs value of one bad ad campaign). Used sparingly in Phase 1, increases in Phase 2 and 3.
what_not_to_do: Do NOT post generic 'we run data-driven ads' or 'grow your business with Meta' content — this is the exact commodity noise that scores 0-3 likes in the scraped feed and would bury OffGrid in the same low-resonance pool
Do NOT use stock images or templated Canva carousels that look like every other agency post — the scraped feed is saturated with this and it signals low authority
Do NOT spend any money on paid ads before Phase 3 — zero-proof paid amplification burns budget and builds no trust; organic proof-building must come first
Do NOT try to sell in DMs before delivering value — every outreach must lead with a free insight or beta offer, not a pitch
Do NOT post more than 20% sales-CTA content in Phase 1 — the ratio should be 80% education/proof, 20% CTA until trust assets are established
Do NOT chase vanity metrics like follower count — optimize for DM conversations, form fills, and testimonial collection

Do NOT create content about 'AI writing ad copy' or general AI hype — the trend data shows this conversation exists but is not specific to Meta Ad Library intelligence; borrowing this framing dilutes OffGrid's unique positioning
trend_angles_to_exploit: STRUCTURAL GAP — Zero content in the scraped feed addresses competitor ad intelligence or Meta Ad Library analysis. OffGrid is the first and only voice in this space. Every piece of content should reinforce this ownership.

AI + ADS VELOCITY — The 'AI applied to ad workflows' signal is gaining momentum but is stuck on AI-for-ad-copy framing. OffGrid reframes this to AI-for-competitive-intelligence, riding the velocity while occupying a distinct subcategory.

FOUNDER FRUSTRATION LANGUAGE — Exact phrases from scraped posts like 'tested ads and they didn't work' and 'bad creatives' and 'wrong targeting' should be mirrored in hooks to create instant recognition and trust.

Q1/Q2 PERFORMANCE RECAP FORMAT — Founders sharing real numbers are getting organic traction. OffGrid should use this format to share anonymized aggregate insights from beta reports ('We analyzed 847 competitor ads across 5 D2C brands. Here's what we found.').

STAT-BACKED BOLD OPENER FORMAT — The carousel format with a single bold stat on the first slide is proven to stop scrolls. OffGrid should use proprietary data from reports as the stat source, making the content unreplicable by competitors.
strategy.competitive_positioning: OffGrid Creatives AI is not another Meta ads agency. It is not an ad creative tool. It is not an AI copywriter. It is the only product that takes the publicly available Meta Ad Library — 10 million active ads — and converts a founder's specific competitive landscape into a structured 17-section intelligence report delivered in under 10 minutes. The positioning is: everyone else tells you to improve your ads from the inside. OffGrid is the only one telling you to look outside — at what's already working for your competitors — and giving you the tool to do it instantly. This maps directly to the structural content gap where zero competitors are speaking to this need.
strategy.conversion_path: Instagram/LinkedIn content (hook from trend data) → Profile visit (bio clearly states 'AI-powered competitor ad intelligence in 10 minutes') → Link in bio to Google Form → Form fill (brand name, competitor names, email) → Make.com triggers Apify scrape → Claude API analysis → Railway Flask generates PDF → Gmail delivers 17-section report to client inbox in under 10 minutes → Follow-up DM/email requesting testimonial → Testimonial becomes content → Content attracts next client. Secondary path: DM outreach to engaged followers or community members → Free beta report offer → Delivery → Testimonial → Conversion of their network via word-of-mouth.
risk_factors: PIPELINE RELIABILITY — The Make.com → Apify → Claude → Railway → Gmail pipeline must work flawlessly for every delivery. A single failed or delayed report during beta phase destroys trust before it's built. Mitigation: test pipeline 5 times with different brands before first beta client, have manual fallback process ready.

META AD LIBRARY ACCESS — Apify scraping of Meta Ad Library is subject to rate limits, structural changes, and potential blocking. Mitigation: monitor scrape success rates weekly, maintain backup scraping approach, be transparent with clients if delays occur.

TESTIMONIAL VELOCITY — The entire Phase 2 and 3 strategy depends on collecting proof in Phase 1. If beta clients receive reports but do not provide testimonials, the content engine stalls. Mitigation: make testimonial request part of delivery flow, offer additional free report for a second competitor in exchange for video testimonial, follow up 3 times.

AUDIENCE SIZE — With zero existing following, organic reach is severely limited in the first 30 days. Mitigation: compensate with direct DM outreach (30/week), community participation, and cross-platform posting to maximize surface area.

PRICE SENSITIVITY — INR 999 is low but still a nonzero commitment from founders who have never heard of OffGrid. Mitigation: frame beta price against cost of one wasted ad set (INR 5000-50000), offer money-back guarantee for beta clients, and let the report quality speak for itself.

FOUNDER BANDWIDTH — This is a beta-phase product with one founder likely doing content, outreach, delivery, and product development simultaneously. Mitigation: batch content creation (one session per week), automate everything possible in the pipeline, and prioritize conversion actions over vanity content.
